  • Models
  • Correlations
  • Estimation
  • Tools
marapendi
  • Components
  • View page source

Components

Physical building blocks of an electrochemical cell, and the per-simulation state that mirrors them.

Component classes are dataclasses that hold geometric and material parameters, together with the correlation methods that turn those parameters (and a state object) into transport and electrochemical quantities. Computation across components is orchestrated by CellModel (see Models).

Cell components: assembly of layers into a fuel cell.

A CellSide groups the porous layers and flow channel of one electrode (anode or cathode). A Cell combines a cathode CellSide, an anode CellSide, and a Membrane.

This module defines the dataclasses and the lists used to loop over their components. Orchestration of the calculations is left to marapendi.model.CellModel.

class marapendi.cell.Cell(ca: ~marapendi.cell.CellSide = <factory>, an: ~marapendi.cell.CellSide = <factory>, membrane: ~marapendi.membrane.Membrane = <factory>, area: float = 1.0, electrical_resistance: float = 0.0)[source]

Bases: object

A cell, combining a cathode, an anode and a membrane.

ca

Cathode.

Type:

CellSide

an

Anode.

Type:

CellSide

membrane

Membrane.

Type:

Membrane

area

Active area (m^2).

Type:

float

an: CellSide
area: float = 1.0
ca: CellSide
electrical_resistance: float = 0.0
membrane: Membrane
class marapendi.cell.CellSide(cl: ~marapendi.catalyst_layers.CatalystLayer = <factory>, gdl: ~marapendi.porous_layers.GasDiffusionLayer = <factory>, mpl: ~marapendi.porous_layers.MicroPorousLayer = <factory>, ch: ~marapendi.flow_channels.FlowChannel = <factory>, has_mpl: bool = False, has_gdl: bool = True, thermal_contact_resistance: float = 0.0)[source]

Bases: object

One side (anode or cathode) of a cell.

cl

Catalyst layer.

Type:

CatalystLayer

gdl

Gas diffusion layer.

Type:

GasDiffusionLayer

mpl

Microporous layer.

Type:

MicroPorousLayer

ch

Flow channel.

Type:

FlowChannel

has_mpl, has_gdl

Whether this side includes a microporous / gas diffusion layer.

Type:

bool

ch: FlowChannel
cl: CatalystLayer
gdl: GasDiffusionLayer
has_gdl: bool = True
has_mpl: bool = False
mpl: MicroPorousLayer
thermal_contact_resistance: float = 0.0

State containers for the reference cell model.

Mirrors the shape of marapendi.cell.Cell (ca/an sides, each with a catalyst layer, GDL/MPL and flow channel, plus a membrane) so that state objects line up 1:1 with the components that own the corresponding static parameters. This is a pure data layer: dataclasses only, no physics.

Iteration helpers (layers, sides, porous_layers) let model code loop over all layers/sides without hardcoding ca/an/cl/gdl everywhere.

class marapendi.state.CatalystLayerState(gas: ~marapendi.gas.GasState = <factory>, temperature: float = None, pressure: float = None, saturation_pressure: float = None, surface_tension: float = None, kinematic_viscosity: float = None, density: float = None, relative_humidity: float = None, liquid_saturation: float = 0.0, non_wetting_saturation: float = 0.0, capillary_pressure: float = None, ionomer_water_content: float = None, overpotential: float = None, crossover_current: float = None, water_film_thickness: float = None, proton_resistance: float = None, theta_catalyst: float = None, eq_water_content: float = None, membrane_interface_water_content: float = None)[source]

Bases: LayerState

State of a catalyst layer, in addition to the generic layer state.

crossover_current: float = None
eq_water_content: float = None
ionomer_water_content: float = None
membrane_interface_water_content: float = None
overpotential: float = None
proton_resistance: float = None
theta_catalyst: float = None
water_film_thickness: float = None
class marapendi.state.CellSideState(cl: ~marapendi.state.CatalystLayerState = <factory>, gdl: ~marapendi.state.LayerState | None = <factory>, mpl: ~marapendi.state.LayerState | None = None, ch: ~marapendi.state.FlowChannelState = <factory>, h2ov_transport_resistance: float = None, reactant_transport_resistance: float = None, h2o_production: float = 0.0, s_relax: float | None = None, membrane_water_flux: float = None, water_flux: float = None, liquid_flux: float = None, vapor_flux: float = None)[source]

Bases: object

State of one side (anode or cathode) of the cell.

ch: FlowChannelState
cl: CatalystLayerState
gdl: LayerState | None
h2o_production: float = 0.0
h2ov_transport_resistance: float = None
property layers: list[LayerState]

All layer states on this side, GDL-to-CL order, including MPL and channel if present.

liquid_flux: float = None
membrane_water_flux: float = None
mpl: LayerState | None = None
property porous_layers: list[LayerState]

All layer states on this side, GDL-to-CL order, including MPL if present.

reactant_transport_resistance: float = None
s_relax: float | None = None
vapor_flux: float = None
water_flux: float = None
class marapendi.state.CellState(ca: ~marapendi.state.CellSideState = <factory>, an: ~marapendi.state.CellSideState = <factory>, membrane: ~marapendi.state.MembraneState = <factory>, current_density: float = None, temperature: float = None, cell_voltage: float = None, E_rev: float = None, eta_act: float = None, eta_ohm: float = None)[source]

Bases: object

Full state of the cell at one operating point.

E_rev: float = None
an: CellSideState
ca: CellSideState
cell_voltage: float = None
current_density: float = None
eta_act: float = None
eta_ohm: float = None
property layers: list[LayerState]

All layer states across both sides (GDL/MPL/CL), GDL-to-CL order plus the membrane.

membrane: MembraneState
property side_layers: list[LayerState]

All layer states across both sides (GDL/MPL/CL), GDL-to-CL order.

property sides: tuple[CellSideState, CellSideState]

(ca, an) side states, for for side in state.sides: ... loops.

temperature: float = None
class marapendi.state.FlowChannelState(gas: ~marapendi.gas.GasState = <factory>, temperature: float = None, pressure: float = None, saturation_pressure: float = None, inlet_gas_flow_rate: float = 1e-12, inlet_liquid_flow_rate: float = None, inlet_liquid_saturation: float = None, inlet_stoichiometry: float = None)[source]

Bases: object

State of a flow channel (anode or cathode).

gas: GasState
inlet_gas_flow_rate: float = 1e-12
inlet_liquid_flow_rate: float = None
inlet_liquid_saturation: float = None
inlet_stoichiometry: float = None
pressure: float = None
saturation_pressure: float = None
temperature: float = None
class marapendi.state.LayerState(gas: ~marapendi.gas.GasState = <factory>, temperature: float = None, pressure: float = None, saturation_pressure: float = None, surface_tension: float = None, kinematic_viscosity: float = None, density: float = None, relative_humidity: float = None, liquid_saturation: float = 0.0, non_wetting_saturation: float = 0.0, capillary_pressure: float = None)[source]

Bases: object

State of a single porous layer (GDL, MPL, catalyst layer, …).

capillary_pressure: float = None
density: float = None

kinematic_viscosity: float = None
liquid_saturation: float = 0.0
non_wetting_saturation: float = 0.0
pressure: float = None
relative_humidity: float = None
saturation_pressure: float = None
surface_tension: float = None
temperature: float = None
class marapendi.state.MembraneState(temperature: float = None, saturation_pressure: float = None, density: float = None, water_content: float = None, water_flux: float = None, h2_permeation_flux: float = None, proton_resistance: float = None, peclet_number: float = None, water_diffusion_resistance: float = None, water_content_profile: float = None, water_content_derivative_profile: float = None)[source]

Bases: object

State of the membrane.

density: float = None
h2_permeation_flux: float = None
peclet_number: float = None
proton_resistance: float = None
saturation_pressure: float = None
temperature: float = None
water_content: float = None
water_content_derivative_profile: float = None
water_content_profile: float = None
water_diffusion_resistance: float = None
water_flux: float = None

Catalyst layer components: static physical properties and correlations.

CatalystLayer and PtCCatalystLayer extend PorousLayer with the electrochemical-reaction and ionomer properties of a catalyst layer.

class marapendi.catalyst_layers.CatalystLayer(thickness: float = 0.001, porosity: float = 1.0, effective_gas_diffusion_ratio: float = 1.0, pore_diameter: float = 1000000000000.0, absolute_permeability: float = 1000000.0, relative_permeability_exponent: float = 3.0, contact_angle: float = 120.0, thermal_conductivity: float = 1000000000000.0, non_wetting_phase: str = 'water', wetting_phase: str = 'gas', transport_resistance_model: PorousGasResistanceModel = <factory>, two_phase_transport_model: DarcyTransportModel = <factory>, ionomer: CatalystLayerIonomer = <factory>, reaction: ElectrochemicalReaction = <factory>, catalyst_loading: float = 0.002, ecsa: float = 70000.0)[source]

Bases: PorousLayer

Generic catalyst layer: an ionomer phase, catalyst particles and reaction sites.

ionomer

Ionomer transport-property correlations.

Type:

CatalystLayerIonomer

reaction

Electrochemical reaction kinetics.

Type:

ElectrochemicalReaction

catalyst_loading

Catalyst loading (kg/m^2).

Type:

float

ecsa

Electrochemically active surface area (m^2/kg catalyst).

Type:

float

catalyst_loading: float = 0.002
ecsa: float = 70000.0
ionomer: CatalystLayerIonomer
reaction: ElectrochemicalReaction
class marapendi.catalyst_layers.PtCCatalystLayer(thickness: float = 1e-05, porosity: float = 1.0, effective_gas_diffusion_ratio: float = 1.0, pore_diameter: float = 1000000000000.0, absolute_permeability: float = 1000000.0, relative_permeability_exponent: float = 3.0, contact_angle: float = 95.0, thermal_conductivity: float = 1000000000000.0, non_wetting_phase: str = 'water', wetting_phase: str = 'gas', transport_resistance_model: PorousGasResistanceModel = <factory>, two_phase_transport_model: DarcyTransportModel = <factory>, ionomer: PFSAIonomer = <factory>, reaction: ElectrochemicalReaction = <factory>, catalyst_loading: float = 0.002, ecsa: float = 70000.0, platinum_loading: float = 0.002, catalyst_platinum_weight_percent: float = 0.5, ionomer_to_carbon_ratio: float = 0.75, platinum_density: float = 21450.0, carbon_density: float = 1950.0, carbon_agglomerate_radius: float = 2.5e-08, omega_PtO: float = 3000000.0, ionomer_k1: float = 8.5, ionomer_k2: float = 5.4, ionomer_k3: float = 5.4)[source]

Bases: CatalystLayer

Pt/C catalyst layer with explicit Pt/carbon/ionomer microstructure.

ionomer

Ionomer transport-property correlations.

Type:

PFSAIonomer

thickness

Catalyst layer thickness (m).

Type:

float

platinum_loading

Platinum loading (kg/m^2).

Type:

float

catalyst_platinum_weight_percent

Platinum weight fraction in the catalyst (Pt/C).

Type:

float

ionomer_to_carbon_ratio

Dry ionomer-to-carbon mass ratio.

Type:

float

platinum_density, carbon_density

Densities of platinum and carbon (kg/m^3).

Type:

float

carbon_agglomerate_radius

Radius of the carbon agglomerates (m).

Type:

float

contact_angle

Contact angle of the wetting phase (degrees).

Type:

float

omega_PtO

Platinum-oxide coverage parameter.

Type:

float

ionomer_k1, ionomer_k2, ionomer_k3

Empirical interfacial-resistance coefficients (Hao et al. 2015).

Type:

float

carbon_agglomerate_radius: float = 2.5e-08
carbon_density: float = 1950.0
catalyst_platinum_weight_percent: float = 0.5
contact_angle: float = 95.0
effective_charge_resistance(current_density: float, ionomer_water_content: float, temperature: float, charge: str = 'proton') → float[source]

Effective charge resistance (Ohm.m^2), from Neyerlin et al. (2007) / Goshtasbi et al. (2020).

ionomer: PFSAIonomer
ionomer_film_thickness(state) → float[source]

Thickness of the ionomer film coating the carbon agglomerates (m).

ionomer_k1: float = 8.5
ionomer_k2: float = 5.4
ionomer_k3: float = 5.4
ionomer_sheet_charge_resistance(ionomer_water_content: float, temperature: float, charge: str = 'proton') → float[source]

Ionomer film charge resistance (Ohm.m^2), with tortuosity from Hao et al. (2016).

ionomer_to_carbon_ratio: float = 0.75
ionomer_vol_fraction(state) → float[source]

Wet ionomer volume fraction, given the catalyst layer state.

ionomer_vol_surface_area(state) → float[source]

Ionomer/gas interfacial area per unit volume (m^2/m^3).

o2_ionomer_film_bulk_resistance(state) → float[source]

Bulk O2 transport resistance through the ionomer film (s/m).

o2_ionomer_film_resistance(state) → float[source]

Total O2 transport resistance through the ionomer/water film (s/m).

Interface resistances from eq. 32 of Hao et al. (2015), neglecting the effect of the water film on the interface terms.

omega_PtO: float = 3000000.0
platinum_density: float = 21450.0
platinum_loading: float = 0.002
thickness: float = 1e-05
water_film_thickness(state) → float[source]

Thickness of the liquid water film around the agglomerates (m).

wet_porosity(state) → float[source]

Porosity of the wet catalyst layer.

Porous layer components: static physical properties and correlations.

A PorousLayer (and its specializations GasDiffusionLayer and MicroPorousLayer) holds only static physical properties (geometry, permeability, wettability, …) plus the correlation models used to turn those properties, together with a LayerState, into transport quantities (gas diffusion resistance, capillary pressure / saturation relations, thermal resistance).

No physical variables (temperature, pressure, gas composition, saturation, …) are stored here – those live in LayerState and are produced/consumed by marapendi.model.CellModel.

class marapendi.porous_layers.GasDiffusionLayer(thickness: float = 0.0002, porosity: float = 0.6, effective_gas_diffusion_ratio: float = 1.0, pore_diameter: float = 1000000000000.0, absolute_permeability: float = 1e-12, relative_permeability_exponent: float = 3.0, contact_angle: float = 120.0, thermal_conductivity: float = 0.5, non_wetting_phase: str = 'water', wetting_phase: str = 'gas', transport_resistance_model: ~marapendi.transport_models.PorousGasResistanceModel = <factory>, two_phase_transport_model: ~marapendi.transport_models.DarcyTransportModel = <factory>)[source]

Bases: PorousLayer

Gas diffusion layer: a PorousLayer with typical GDL defaults.

absolute_permeability: float = 1e-12
contact_angle: float = 120.0
porosity: float = 0.6
thermal_conductivity: float = 0.5
thickness: float = 0.0002
class marapendi.porous_layers.MicroPorousLayer(thickness: float = 3e-05, porosity: float = 0.4, effective_gas_diffusion_ratio: float = 1.0, pore_diameter: float = 1000000000000.0, absolute_permeability: float = 1e-13, relative_permeability_exponent: float = 3.0, contact_angle: float = 130.0, thermal_conductivity: float = 0.3, non_wetting_phase: str = 'water', wetting_phase: str = 'gas', transport_resistance_model: ~marapendi.transport_models.PorousGasResistanceModel = <factory>, two_phase_transport_model: ~marapendi.transport_models.DarcyTransportModel = <factory>)[source]

Bases: PorousLayer

Microporous layer (MPL): a PorousLayer with typical MPL defaults.

absolute_permeability: float = 1e-13
contact_angle: float = 130.0
porosity: float = 0.4
thermal_conductivity: float = 0.3
thickness: float = 3e-05
class marapendi.porous_layers.PorousLayer(thickness: float = 0.001, porosity: float = 1.0, effective_gas_diffusion_ratio: float = 1.0, pore_diameter: float = 1000000000000.0, absolute_permeability: float = 1000000.0, relative_permeability_exponent: float = 3.0, contact_angle: float = 120.0, thermal_conductivity: float = 1000000000000.0, non_wetting_phase: str = 'water', wetting_phase: str = 'gas', transport_resistance_model: ~marapendi.transport_models.PorousGasResistanceModel = <factory>, two_phase_transport_model: ~marapendi.transport_models.DarcyTransportModel = <factory>)[source]

Bases: object

Static properties of a porous layer (GDL, MPL or catalyst layer).

thickness

Layer thickness (m).

Type:

float

porosity

Layer porosity (-).

Type:

float

effective_gas_diffusion_ratio

Ratio of the effective to bulk gas diffusivity (-).

Type:

float

pore_diameter

Mean pore diameter (m), used for Knudsen diffusion.

Type:

float

absolute_permeability

Absolute (intrinsic) permeability (m^2).

Type:

float

relative_permeability_exponent

Exponent of the relative permeability law (-).

Type:

float

contact_angle

Contact angle of the wetting phase (degrees).

Type:

float

thermal_conductivity

Thermal conductivity (W/m/K).

Type:

float

non_wetting_phase, wetting_phase

Identity of the two fluid phases (‘water’ or ‘gas’).

Type:

str

transport_resistance_model

Correlation used for gas diffusion resistance.

Type:

PorousGasResistanceModel

two_phase_transport_model

Correlation used for the capillary-pressure / saturation relation.

Type:

DarcyTransportModel

absolute_permeability: float = 1000000.0
capillary_pressure_J_ratio(state) → float[source]

Capillary-pressure scale of the layer’s J-function, given the layer’s state.

capillary_pressure_from_saturation(state, non_wetting_saturation: float) → float[source]

Capillary pressure from non_wetting_saturation, given the layer’s state.

contact_angle: float = 120.0
effective_gas_diffusion_ratio: float = 1.0
gas_transport_resistance(state, species: str = 'o2') → float[source]

Gas diffusion resistance for species (s/m), given the layer’s state.

non_wetting_phase: str = 'water'
non_wetting_saturation_from_flux(state, non_wetting_flux: float, upstream_capillary_pressure: float = 0.0) → tuple[float, float][source]

Non-wetting (liquid water) saturation and downstream capillary pressure.

Vectorized, unmasked variant of marapendi.transport_models.DarcyTransportModel.calculate_non_wetting_saturation(), from the non-wetting phase flux through the layer and the capillary pressure at its upstream boundary.

Returns:

  • average_saturation (float) – Saturation averaged across the layer.

  • downstream_capillary_pressure (float) – Capillary pressure at the downstream boundary, for chaining into the next layer’s upstream_capillary_pressure.

pore_diameter: float = 1000000000000.0
porosity: float = 1.0
relative_permeability_exponent: float = 3.0
saturation_flow_resistance(state) → float[source]

Resistance to non-wetting phase flow due to a saturation gradient (s.m^2/mol).

saturation_from_capillary_pressure(state, capillary_pressure: float) → float[source]

Non-wetting phase saturation from capillary_pressure, given the layer’s state.

thermal_conductivity: float = 1000000000000.0
thermal_resistance() → float[source]

Thermal resistance of the layer (m^2 K / W).

thickness: float = 0.001
transport_resistance_model: PorousGasResistanceModel
two_phase_transport_model: DarcyTransportModel
wetting_phase: str = 'gas'

Membrane components: static physical properties and correlations.

A Membrane (and specializations such as PFSA) holds the static physical properties of the membrane (equivalent weight, density, thickness, transport-property correlations, …). It inherits all the generic ionomer correlations from Ionomer and adds membrane-specific properties (dry thickness, hydrogen permeation, sorption isotherms, proton conductivity).

Physical variables (water content, temperature, fluxes, …) live in marapendi.state.MembraneState and are produced/consumed by marapendi.water_balance.MembraneWaterBalanceModel.

class marapendi.membrane.Membrane(equivalent_weight: float = 1100.0, dry_density: float = 1980.0, reference_water_diffusivity: float = 4.3e-10, reference_absorption_coefficient: float = 1e-05, reference_temperature: float = 353.15, water_diffusivity_activation_energy: float = 20000000.0, water_absorption_activation_energy: float = 20000000.0, dry_thickness: float = 2.5e-05, h2_permeation_model: ~marapendi.membrane_permeation_models.HydrogenPermeationModel = <factory>)[source]

Bases: Ionomer

Static properties of a proton/anion exchange membrane.

dry_thickness

Membrane thickness (m).

Type:

float

h2_permeation_model

Correlation used for the hydrogen permeation flux.

Type:

HydrogenPermeationModel

dry_thickness: float = 2.5e-05
h2_permeation_model: HydrogenPermeationModel
hydrogen_permeation_flux(partial_pressure_h2: float, temperature: float, pressure_difference: float, water_vol_fraction: float) → float[source]

Hydrogen permeation flux through the membrane (kmol/m^2/s).

class marapendi.membrane.PFSA(equivalent_weight: float = 1100.0, dry_density: float = 1980.0, reference_water_diffusivity: float = 4.3e-10, reference_absorption_coefficient: float = 1e-05, reference_temperature: float = 353.15, water_diffusivity_activation_energy: float = 20000000.0, water_absorption_activation_energy: float = 20000000.0, dry_thickness: float = 2.5e-05, h2_permeation_model: ~marapendi.membrane_permeation_models.HydrogenPermeationModel = <factory>, conductivity_correction: float = 1.0, conductivity_exp: float = 1.5, conductivity_activation_energy: float = 15000000.0, relaxation_water_content_fraction: float = 0.15)[source]

Bases: Membrane

Perfluorosulfonic-acid (PFSA, e.g. Nafion) membrane.

conductivity_correction

Correction factor for the proton conductivity correlation (Vetter and Schumacher, 2020).

Type:

float

conductivity_exp

Exponent of the proton conductivity correlation.

Type:

float

conductivity_activation_energy

Activation energy for proton conductivity (J/kmol).

Type:

float

relaxation_water_content_fraction

Fraction of the equilibrium water content that relaxes towards s_relax rather than the Springer isotherm (Goshtasbi et al., 2019).

Type:

float

References

Springer, T. E. et al. J. Electrochem. Soc. 138, 2334 (1991). Goshtasbi, A. et al. J. Electrochem. Soc. 167, 024518 (2020).

conductivity_activation_energy: float = 15000000.0
conductivity_correction: float = 1.0
conductivity_exp: float = 1.5
equilibrium_water_content(rh: float, temperature: float, s_relax: float | None = None) → float[source]

Equilibrium water content (mol H2O / mol SO3-) from the Springer et al. (1991) isotherm.

If s_relax is given (Goshtasbi et al. 2019 dynamic relaxation term), the isotherm value is scaled down by relaxation_water_content_fraction and s_relax is added back; otherwise the raw isotherm value is returned (steady-state).

equilibrium_water_content_derivative(rh: float, temperature: float, s_relax: float | None = None) → float[source]

Derivative of equilibrium_water_content() with respect to relative humidity.

liquid_equilibrium_water_content(temperature: float) → float[source]

Equilibrium water content (mol H2O / mol SO3-) in contact with liquid water.

Reference

Goshtasbi, A. et al. J. Electrochem. Soc. 167, 024518 (2020).

proton_conductivity(water_content_profile: float, temperature: float) → float[source]

Through-plane proton conductivity (S/m) from a membrane water content profile.

proton_resistance(water_content_profile: float, temperature: float) → float[source]

Through-plane proton resistance (Ohm.m^2) from a membrane water content profile.

relaxation_water_content_fraction: float = 0.15

Flow channel components: static physical properties and correlations.

FlowChannel extends PorousLayer with the geometric properties of a gas flow channel.

class marapendi.flow_channels.FlowChannel(thickness: float = 0.001, porosity: float = 1.0, effective_gas_diffusion_ratio: float = 1.0, pore_diameter: float = 1000000000000.0, absolute_permeability: float = 1000000.0, relative_permeability_exponent: float = 3.0, contact_angle: float = 120.0, thermal_conductivity: float = 1000000000000.0, non_wetting_phase: str = 'water', wetting_phase: str = 'gas', transport_resistance_model: ChannelGasResistanceModel = <factory>, two_phase_transport_model: DarcyTransportModel = <factory>, reactant: str = 'o2', width: float = 0.001, height: float = 0.001, length: float = 0.1, channel_land_ratio: float = 1.0, n_parallel: int = 14)[source]

Bases: PorousLayer

Flow channel geometry of a single fuel cell side.

reactant

Primary reactant species (‘o2’ or ‘h2’).

Type:

str

width, height, length

Channel cross-section width, height and length (m).

Type:

float

channel_land_ratio

Ratio of channel width to land width.

Type:

float

n_parallel

Number of parallel channels.

Type:

int

transport_resistance_model

Correlation used for the channel gas transport resistance.

Type:

ChannelGasResistanceModel

channel_land_ratio: float = 1.0
gas_transport_resistance(state, species: str = 'o2') → float[source]

Gas transport resistance for species (s/m), given the channel’s state.

height: float = 0.001
length: float = 0.1
n_parallel: int = 14
reactant: str = 'o2'
transport_resistance_model: ChannelGasResistanceModel
width: float = 0.001

Ionomer material properties and correlations.

Ionomer holds the dry material properties of an ion-exchange polymer (equivalent weight, density, water-transport correlations) and the correlation methods that depend only on those properties together with water_content/temperature. Membrane inherits from it and adds membrane-specific geometry (thickness, hydrogen permeation, …).

CatalystLayerIonomer and its specializations (PFSAIonomer, PAPIonomer) extend Ionomer with the charge-transport correlations needed by a catalyst layer’s ionomer phase.

class marapendi.ionomer.CatalystLayerIonomer(equivalent_weight: float = 952.0, dry_density: float = 2004.0, reference_water_diffusivity: float = 4.3e-10, reference_absorption_coefficient: float = 1e-05, reference_temperature: float = 353.15, water_diffusivity_activation_energy: float = 20000000.0, water_absorption_activation_energy: float = 20000000.0)[source]

Bases: Ionomer

Base class for the ionomer phase of a catalyst layer.

dry_density

Dry density of the ionomer (kg/m^3).

Type:

float

equivalent_weight

Equivalent weight of the ionomer (kg/kmol).

Type:

float

charge_conductivity(water_content: float, temperature: float, charge: str = 'proton') → float[source]

Charge conductivity (proton or hydroxide), in S/m.

dry_density: float = 2004.0
equivalent_weight: float = 952.0
class marapendi.ionomer.Ionomer(equivalent_weight: float = 1100.0, dry_density: float = 1980.0, reference_water_diffusivity: float = 4.3e-10, reference_absorption_coefficient: float = 1e-05, reference_temperature: float = 353.15, water_diffusivity_activation_energy: float = 20000000.0, water_absorption_activation_energy: float = 20000000.0)[source]

Bases: object

Dry material properties of an ion-exchange polymer.

equivalent_weight

Equivalent weight (kg/kmol).

Type:

float

dry_density

Dry density (kg/m^3).

Type:

float

reference_water_diffusivity

Reference adsorbed-water diffusivity (m^2/s).

Type:

float

reference_absorption_coefficient

Reference water absorption coefficient (m/s).

Type:

float

reference_temperature

Reference temperature for the Arrhenius corrections (K).

Type:

float

water_diffusivity_activation_energy, water_absorption_activation_energy

Activation energies for water diffusivity / absorption (J/kmol).

Type:

float

calculate_electroosmotic_drag_coefficient(temperature: float, water_content: float) → float[source]

Electroosmotic drag coefficient (n.d.) for a given water_content.

calculate_electroosmotic_drag_speed(temperature: float, current_density: float) → float[source]

Electroosmotic drag speed (m/s) for a given current_density (A/m^2).

calculate_water_absorption_coefficient(temperature: float) → float[source]

Water absorption coefficient (m/s) at temperature.

calculate_water_diffusivity(temperature: float) → float[source]

Adsorbed water diffusivity (m^2/s) at temperature.

dry_density: float = 1980.0
equivalent_weight: float = 1100.0
reference_absorption_coefficient: float = 1e-05
reference_temperature: float = 353.15
reference_water_diffusivity: float = 4.3e-10
water_absorption_activation_energy: float = 20000000.0
water_diffusivity_activation_energy: float = 20000000.0
water_vol_fraction(water_content: float, water_molar_volume: float) → float[source]

Volume fraction of water in the ionomer, for a given water_content (mol H2O / mol SO3-).

wet_density(water_content: float, temperature: float) → float[source]

Wet density of the ionomer (kg/m^3).

wet_expansion_factor(water_content: float, temperature: float) → float[source]

Volumetric expansion factor due to water uptake, relative to the dry volume.

class marapendi.ionomer.PAPIonomer(equivalent_weight: float = 425.531914893617, dry_density: float = 1220.0, reference_water_diffusivity: float = 4.3e-10, reference_absorption_coefficient: float = 1e-05, reference_temperature: float = 353.15, water_diffusivity_activation_energy: float = 20000000.0, water_absorption_activation_energy: float = 20000000.0)[source]

Bases: CatalystLayerIonomer

Poly(aryl piperidinium) (PAP) ionomer.

dry_density

Density of the ionomer (kg/m^3). Data from Luo et al. (2020), table 1.

Type:

float

equivalent_weight

Equivalent weight of the ionomer (kg/kmol).

Type:

float

References

Eon Chae, J. et al. J. Ind. Eng. Chem. 133, 255-262 (2024) Luo, X. et al. J. Memb. Sci. 598, 117680 (2020) Khalid, H. et al. Membranes (Basel) 12, 989 (2022).

dry_density: float = 1220.0
equivalent_weight: float = 425.531914893617
hydroxide_conductivity(water_content: float, temperature: float) → float[source]

Hydroxide conductivity (S/m).

Room-temperature conductivity for liquid-equilibrated PAP-20 from Luo et al. (2020), with activation energy from Khalid et al. (2022).

References

Luo, X. et al. J. Memb. Sci. 598, 117680 (2020) Khalid, H. et al. Membranes (Basel) 12, 989 (2022).

class marapendi.ionomer.PFSAIonomer(equivalent_weight: float = 952.0, dry_density: float = 2004.0, reference_water_diffusivity: float = 4.3e-10, reference_absorption_coefficient: float = 1e-05, reference_temperature: float = 353.15, water_diffusivity_activation_energy: float = 20000000.0, water_absorption_activation_energy: float = 20000000.0, conductivity_correction: float = 1, conductivity_exp: float = 1.5, hydrated_proton_conductivity: float = 11, conductivity_activation_energy: float = 11000000.0, hydrated_o2_diffusion: float = 7.430439362572494e-10, o2_diffusion_exponent: float = 0.708, o2_diffusion_activation_energy: float = 24000000.0)[source]

Bases: CatalystLayerIonomer

PFSA ionomer (e.g. Nafion) with empirical fits for proton conductivity and O2 transport.

conductivity_activation_energy: float = 11000000.0
conductivity_correction: float = 1
conductivity_exp: float = 1.5
equilibrium_water_content(rh: float) → float[source]

Equilibrium water content as a function of relative humidity.

From Jinnouchi et al. (2021), fig S3a.

References

Jinnouchi, R. et al. Nat. Commun. 12, 4956 (2021).

hydrated_o2_diffusion: float = 7.430439362572494e-10
hydrated_proton_conductivity: float = 11
hydroxide_conductivity(water_content: float, temperature: float) → float[source]

Hydroxide conductivity (S/m). PFSA ionomers do not conduct hydroxide.

o2_diffusion_activation_energy: float = 24000000.0
o2_diffusion_exponent: float = 0.708
o2_film_diffusion_coefficient(water_content: float, temperature: float = 353.15) → float[source]

Effective O2 diffusion coefficient in the hydrated ionomer film (m^2/s).

o2_permeability(water_content: float, temperature: float = 353.15) → float[source]

O2 permeability (kmol/m/s/Pa) from a volume-fraction approach.

Data from Goshtasbi et al. (2020).

References

Goshtasbi, A. et al. J. Electrochem. Soc. 167, 024518 (2020).

proton_conductivity(water_content: float, temperature: float) → float[source]

Proton conductivity from empirical fits (S/m).
